Committee.CommitteeName: Subcommittee on Appropriations - Agriculture, Conservation, Natural Resources, and Economic Development
Subcommittee on Appropriations - Health, Mental Health, and Social Services

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 113, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Mitch Boggs, a Republican, represents Lawrence County (District 157) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2020.
Born in Carthage, he resides in La Russell with his wife, Lynn. They are proud parents of eight children and grandparents to 12.
A dedicated public servant, Boggs is a volunteer firefighter, president of the Avilla Fire District Board, and the Vineyard Township Road District Commissioner.
With a lifelong career in construction, he owns a construction business in addition to owning and operating Exit 33 Truck Wash with Lynn.
A cattle farmer and licensed pilot, Boggs has a deep love for aviation and serves the Lord as a preacher in his local church.
His family enjoys rodeos and living out their cowboy heritage, blending tradition and service in every aspect of their lives.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to the placement of any surveillance or game camera on private property
Establishes provisions relating to covenant marriages
Establishes protections for landowners taking wildlife on the landowner's property
Creates the "Caller ID Anti-Spoofing Act" to protect businesses and residents from unwanted solicitation from misleading or misidentified solicitors
Modifies a provision relating to liability for equine or livestock activities
Establishes provisions relating to inquiries of active warrants prior to release from a mental health facility
Establishes the "Promoting a Return to Work Act of 2025", modifying provisions relating to work search activities for unemployment compensation claimants

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to home school protections
Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Requires signature verification of absentee ballots
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Modifies provisions relating to electronic communications
Prohibits fire protection district boards from enacting ordinances that subject agriculture buildings to certain fire safety requirements
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Creates a requirement that users must provide government-issued identification prior to viewing pornographic materials provided by commercial entities on the internet
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions relating to constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions relating to constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to modification of the Constitution
